payload:
SOLE Research Loop — self-organizing experiment pipeline.

ARCHITECTURE: SOLE (Structurally Orthogonal Latent Experts). All-modules LoRA, rank-16.
STATE: Read .ralph/current_direction.md for active work. Read HYPOTHESES.yml for full roadmap.
CONTEXT: See VISION.md (architecture), FINDINGS.md (proven results).

HOW TO PICK WORK:
1. Read HYPOTHESES.yml
2. Find all `open` nodes whose `depends_on` are ALL satisfied (status `proven` or `supported`)
3. If your prompt specifies a SCALE CONSTRAINT (e.... [truncated, 2320 chars total]

exp_expert_pruning_from_composition: MATH.md + SPEC.md ready at macro/expert_pruning_composition/. Budget ~/bin/zsh.24 (90min A5000). 8 phases: LOO ranking, PPL/MMLU accumulation curves, greedy forward selection, bottom-K removal, stability. Key insight: formal bound shows pruning benefit ~0.6% at SOLE cosines, right at K1 boundary — likely KILLED (more is always better).

task_id: run_expert_pruning_1773709930, experiment: exp_expert_pruning_from_composition, script: macro/expert_pruning_composition/run_expert_pruning.py, queue_position: 4th (3 pending ahead), completed_tasks: eval_math500 FAILED, run_mmlu_eval OK

active: run_reasoning_distillation_1773709133 (re-run, 14400s timeout, GPU 88%), pending: 4 tasks (individual_eval, leave_one_out, dropout_robustness, expert_pruning). MMLU eval already harvested. eval_math500+interference failures root-caused to rsync --delete nuking adapter — current re-run chains all phases so will self-heal.
